persona.name: Travis Olson

occupation.title: Lead Journeyman Electrician
occupation.organization: IBEW Local 55 / Heartland Electrical Contractors, Des Moines, IA
occupation.description: Leads a 4-person union electrical crew on commercial and industrial job sites in the Des Moines metro. Reads electrical drawings, manages material pulls, and mentors second-year apprentices. Work quality is taken seriously — a wiring error at scale is dangerous, and he knows it. Applies the same exacting standard to every purchase: does it do what it's supposed to do, reliably, for the expected cost?

persona.education: IBEW Local 55 apprenticeship program (4-year), graduated journeyman; some community college credits in business math
long_term_goals: Get his master electrician license and eventually open a small independent shop
Build the garage workshop he has been planning since they bought the house
Jog consistently long enough to run a local 5K without embarrassing himself
Pay off the house 8 years early
persona.style: Guarded and blunt — gives short, direct answers unless asked to elaborate. Does not perform enthusiasm about anything. Evaluates products the way he evaluates electrical installations: does it meet spec, how long will it last, is the cost proportional to what it delivers? Physically broad; dresses for function and cleanliness but not presentation. Has strong opinions about things that matter to him and almost no opinion about things that don't.

traits: Deeply skeptical of premium pricing that can't justify itself functionally
Consistent and reliable — same schedule, same routes, same brand unless there's a reason to change
Low openness to new products; requires demonstrated evidence of improvement
Not hostile, just unmoved by things he doesn't find credible

interests: Jogging 2–3 times per week as health maintenance
Hunting and fishing (whitetail in November, walleye April through summer)
Iowa Hawkeyes football — season tickets for two years running
Woodworking — currently building a coffee table
likes: Shoes that hold up for a full year of twice-weekly jogging without structural breakdown
New Balance and Brooks — bought one pair of each, both worked
Clear, simple product descriptions that tell him what the shoe does
Honest return policies (he hasn't needed them but he reads them)
dislikes: Sneaker culture — finds the idea of owning 14 pairs of shoes confusing and faintly absurd
Fashion-branded athletic shoes that charge a premium for aesthetics he doesn't value
Excessive colorways — 'I'm running in Iowa in November, who's looking at my shoes'
Any shoe release described as a 'collab' with a celebrity or artist
Review content from people who don't actually run in the shoes they're reviewing

beliefs: Pay for function, not a logo or a designer's name
Less is more — cut the noise and tell me what it does
Quality speaks for itself; it doesn't need marketing
If you're spending more than double the base price for the same outcome, you're being played
skills: Commercial and industrial electrical installation and troubleshooting
Union crew management and apprentice mentorship

general: Jogs Monday, Wednesday, and Friday evenings after work — same 3.5-mile neighborhood loop
Has bought the same NB 860 model twice; will buy it a third time unless it gets discontinued
Reads two or three actual consumer reviews before a purchase, not ten — wants signal, not noise
Will not buy a shoe that costs more than $130 unless someone he trusts explains specifically why it's worth it

morning: Coffee, check weather and job site notes
workday: Crew check-ins and material management throughout the day
evening: Jog Mon/Wed/Fri after a 30-minute sit-down from work
Woodworking in garage or Hawkeye game if in season
weekend: Hunting or fishing Saturday depending on season
Extended family Sunday; his parents are 40 minutes away in Boone

health.physical: Physically strong from trade work. Managing mild tendinitis in his right knee — a recurring issue from 10 years of kneeling on concrete. Started jogging on his doctor's recommendation for cardiovascular health; has lost 11 pounds over the last year. Wears a knee sleeve on longer jogs.
health.mental: Stable and uncomplicated. Jogging is routine maintenance, not a passion — he approaches it the way he approaches changing the oil in his truck. The absence of a fuss about it is the point. Ashley has noticed he's less irritable on jogging days, which he acknowledges when directly asked.

relationships.name: Ashley Olson
relationships.description: Dental hygienist at a Ankeny family practice. Shares Travis's financial pragmatism. She was the one who pointed out his NB 860s were wearing unevenly and suggested he replace them before injury.

relationships.name: Derek
relationships.relation: Union co-worker and occasional jogging partner
relationships.description: Journeyman on Travis's crew. They jog together Wednesday evenings after work — mostly silent, which is precisely how both of them prefer it.

relationships.name: Rob
relationships.description: Grew up three houses down from Travis in Boone. They have been hunting whitetail together every November since they were 16. Rob is the only person whose gear recommendations Travis takes at face value.
